headline: Why The Skanska (OM:SKA B) Investment Narrative Is Shifting On Subtle Valuation Tweaks
summary: Skanska’s updated analyst narrative centers on a small SEK 1 shift in the price target, with the fair value estimate holding at SEK 273.33. Analysts link this move to refreshed discount rate assumptions and fine tuning of long term earnings expectations, rather than a change in how they view the business overall. As you read on, you will see how this seemingly minor adjustment fits into the broader story and what to watch as the narrative evolves. Stay updated as the Fair Value for Skanska...

headline: Morgan Stanley, Cliffwater Limit Private Credit Redemptions
summary: Morgan Stanley and Cliffwater capped withdrawals from their multibillion-dollar private credit funds after investors sought to redeem vastly more than the vehicles allow. Bloomberg's Sinead Cruise reports.

content.title: Morgan Stanley, Cliffwater Limit Private Credit Redemptions

headline: Morgan Stanley Private Credit Fund Hit With Redemption Requests
summary: A Morgan Stanley private credit fund is among the latest to say it won’t give back all the money to investors wanting out.  The bank’s North Haven private-income fund in a letter to investors Wednesday said it received quarterly requests making up around 10.9% of the fund’s $7.6 billion in assets, but would stick to a 5% limit.  In the letter, Morgan Stanley said the fund wasn’t designed to offer full liquidity because of the nature of its investments, and that credit fundamentals across the underlying portfolio have been broadly stable.

headline: Honda downgraded to Equal Weight from Overweight at Morgan Stanley
summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Hiroto Segawa downgraded Honda (HMC) to Equal Weight from Overweight with a price target of 1,600 yen, down from 2,000 yen. The firm recommends “selective stock-picking” in Japan’s auto and mobility group, saying industry headwinds are picking up amid rising raw material costs and emerging geopolitical risk. For Honda, the analyst we thinks the re-evaluation of its automobile business will take time. Suzuki remains Morgan Stanley’s Top Pick.Claim 70% Off TipRanks PremiumUn
